raw_title: Law Enforcement and the Public: Designing Effective Public Safety Crowdsourcing Initiatives
raw_abstract: ABSTRACT For law enforcement agencies tasked with keeping citizens safe, the decentralized nature of the Internet presents both structural and cultural challenges. At the same time, advances in information and communication technologies (ICT) have expanded the capacity of law enforcement to monitor and respond to criminal activity in both online and physical environments. This paper explores how crowdsourcing initiatives, as a form of online collective intelligence, can support law enforcement. Drawing on a systematic literature review, the paper examines the design elements of these initivates and, using a morphological approach, presents a conceptual framework for designing and assessing such initatives. In doing so, the paper highlights the potential for crowdsourcing law enforcement initiatives to improve public services, foster policy innovation, enhance citizen engagement and strengthen government legitimacy.
title_en: Law Enforcement and the Public: Designing Effective Public Safety Crowdsourcing Initiatives
title_zh: 执法与公众：设计有效的公共安全众包倡议
abstract_en: ABSTRACT For law enforcement agencies tasked with keeping citizens safe, the decentralized nature of the Internet presents both structural and cultural challenges. At the same time, advances in information and communication technologies (ICT) have expanded the capacity of law enforcement to monitor and respond to criminal activity in both online and physical environments. This paper explores how crowdsourcing initiatives, as a form of online collective intelligence, can support law enforcement. Drawing on a systematic literature review, the paper examines the design elements of these initivates and, using a morphological approach, presents a conceptual framework for designing and assessing such initatives. In doing so, the paper highlights the potential for crowdsourcing law enforcement initiatives to improve public services, foster policy innovation, enhance citizen engagement and strengthen government legitimacy.
abstract_zh: 摘要 对于负责保护公民安全的执法机构而言，互联网的去中心化性质带来了结构性和文化上的挑战。与此同时，信息和通信技术（ICT）的进步扩大了执法机构在线上和物理环境中监控和应对犯罪活动的能力。本文探讨了众包倡议作为一种在线集体智慧形式如何支持执法。通过系统文献综述，本文研究了这些倡议的设计要素，并采用形态学方法提出了设计和评估此类倡议的概念框架。在此过程中，本文强调了众包执法倡议在改善公共服务、促进政策创新、增强公民参与和加强政府合法性方面的潜力。
plain_summary_zh: 这篇论文研究如何利用众包（让公众在线参与）来帮助警察和执法机构更好地保护公共安全。互联网虽然给执法带来挑战，但新技术也让执法更高效。文章通过分析现有研究，提出了一个设计众包执法项目的框架，并指出这样做可以改善服务、鼓励创新、让公民更多参与，并增强政府的公信力。
plain_summary_en: This paper explores how crowdsourcing (engaging the public online) can help police and law enforcement agencies improve public safety. While the internet poses challenges for law enforcement, new technologies also make enforcement more effective. By analyzing existing research, the article presents a framework for designing crowdsourcing law enforcement projects and notes that this can enhance services, foster innovation, increase citizen involvement, and strengthen government trust.
research_direction_zh: 研究数字时代下公众参与执法和公共安全治理的创新模式，特别是通过技术手段促进警民合作与政策优化。
research_direction_en: Research on innovative models of public participation in law enforcement and public safety governance in the digital age, especially through technological means to promote police-citizen collaboration and policy optimization.
